cards.front: What does 4:1 stand for in digital design?
What is the function of an 8:1 demultiplexer?
cards.back: 8:1 demultiplexer is a digital circuit that can select one out of 8 input lines and direct it to one of the outputs

cards.front: What is the difference between a 4:1 and 8:1 demultiplexer?
cards.back: A 4:1 demultiplexer can select one out of 4 input lines, while an 8:1 demultiplexer can select one out of 8 input lines

cards.front: What is the purpose of a 4:1 bus selector?
cards.back: 4:1 bus selector is used to divide a bus into two smaller buses, one for each half of the bus

cards.front: What is the function of an 8:1 bus selector?
cards.back: 8:1 bus selector is used to divide a bus into 8 smaller buses, one for each bit of the bus
